title: Censored AFT model for laps-until-next-stop, then convert to next-lap risk
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem, not just a one-step binary label.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem, not just a one-step binary label. XGBoost AFT explicitly supports right-censoring, which fits rows where the next stop is not observed before race end; that should use more signal from long non-stop stretches than plain binary classification.
implementation_hint: Create a per-row label for laps until the driver’s next PitStop within the same race. Use uncensored [d,d] when a future stop exists and right-censored [remaining_laps,+inf) when it does not. Train `xgboost.train` with `survival:aft`, then transform predicted survival time into `P(stop in 1 lap)` and blend with the current binary GBDT.
expected_effect: Better late-race and long-stint discrimination, especially where the binary model now compresses many non-stop rows together.
risk: Target construction is easy to leak if future stops are computed before fold splitting; all censoring labels must be built fold-safely inside race-blocked CV.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
